pattern: Combining two compressed spatial structures never requires decompressing them: a saturating leaf on either side decides a whole region instantly, and only where both sides are internal do you descend in lockstep.
hints: Expanding both trees back into full grids works, but the compressed form already tells you the answer for huge regions in a single node.
For OR, a leaf carrying one dominates the whole region no matter what the other tree holds there, while a leaf carrying zero contributes nothing.
Recurse quadrant by quadrant when both nodes are internal, and if the four combined children are leaves with equal values, fold them into one leaf.

edge_inputs.description: two 4x4 grids lit in opposite corners; the OR keeps both lit quadrants
two single leaves, one against zero
the OR of two non-uniform 2x2 grids saturates the square — solutions that never merge after recursing return a bogus internal node
one side is a single all-ones leaf while the other is a deep tree — the leaf must win without any descent
one side is a single all-zeros leaf while the other is a deep tree — the result is exactly the other tree
trees of different depths over the same 4x4 region, one collapsed to a leaf per quadrant and one fully expanded in a quadrant
ORing a region of zeros on both sides must still collapse to a single zero leaf rather than four zero leaves

stress.description: pairs of random biased power-of-two grids converted to collapsed quad-tree wire form by an inline helper
